'''Jimmy Beckett''' (born 29 August 1999) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]], on loan from [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the RFL Championship over two separate spells at the club. Beckett spent time on loan from Featherstone at the [[Keighley Cougars]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and the [[Dewsbury Rams]]. He later joined Dewsbury on a permanent deal.<ref>[https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/jimmy-beckett-9828.html itsrugby profile]</ref>

==Background==
Beckett developed his rugby league career in lower leagues, earning a reputation as a powerful and hardworking front-row forward whilst at the [[Featherstone Lions]].

==Playing career==
===Featherstone Rovers===
Beckett spent multiple spells and established himself as a key forward, making 31 appearances and the [[2025 RFL Championship|2025 season]] and helping the club reach the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] play-offs and the [[2025 RFL 1895 Cup#Final|1895 Cup final]].<ref>[https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-news-big-future-predicted-for-prop-signing-following-move-from-featherstone-rovers-5464431 Castleford Tigers news: big future predicted for prop signing following move from Featherstone Rovers]</ref>

He played on loan at the [[Keighley Cougars]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and the [[Dewsbury Rams]], gaining experience across [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[RFL League 1|League 1]] competitions.<ref>[https://dewsburyrams.co.uk/getting-to-know-jimmy-beckett/ Getting to know Jimmy Beckett]</ref><ref>[https://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/158/roughyeds/141636/rams-sign-oldham-promotion-hero-ahead-of-bower-fold-showdown Rams sign Oldham promotion hero ahead of Bower Fold showdown]</ref><ref>[https://www.notreallyheremedia.com/news/sport/beckett-returns-to-oldham-on-season-long-loan/ Beckett returns to Oldham on season-long loan]</ref><ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jimmy-beckett-to-stay-at-keighley-for-remainder-of-2019#:~:text=Keighley%20have%20announced%20that%20they,victory%20over%20the%20Coventry%20Bears. Jimmy Beckett to stay at Keighley for remainder of 2019]</ref><ref>[https://www.dewsburyreporter.co.uk/sport/other-sport/dewsbury-rams-sign-jimmy-beckett-on-loan-from-featherstone-rovers-3198675 Dewsbury Rams sign Jimmy Beckett on loan from Featherstone Rovers]</ref>

Beckett's performances with Featherstone in the Championship attracted attention from [[Super League]] clubs. His robust carries, defensive work rate and consistency made him one of the standout forwards in the second tier of English rugby league.

===Castleford Tigers===
In January 2026, Beckett signed with [[Castleford Tigers]] for the upcoming Super League [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|season]]. The move marked his first contract in a full-time professional environment. Beckett expressed excitement about progressing his career at the top level and adapting to the demands of Super League football.

Castleford's director of rugby [[Chris Chester (rugby league)|Chris Chester]] praised Beckett's work ethic and potential, noting that his strong 2025 campaign made him a valuable addition to the Tigers' forward pack.

In pre-season, Beckett linked up with his new teammates and began preparations for his Super League debut. Castleford's coaching staff highlighted his physicality and dedication during early training sessions.

====Goole Vikings (loan)====
On 1 April 2026, it was announced that he had signed for [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=1 Apr 2026|author=Aaron Bower|title=Castleford Tigers and Warrington Wolves men make Championship loan switch|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-and-warrington-wolves-men-make-championship-loan-switch}}</ref>

====Sheffield Eagles (loan)====
On 9 April 2026, it was announced that he had signed for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=9 Apr 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Castleford Tigers recall forward Jimmy Beckett as fresh Championship loan secured|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jimmy-beckett-castleford-loan-09-04-2026}}</ref>

==== Batley Bulldogs (loan) ====
On 3 June 2026, Beckett joined [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the Championship on an initial one-month loan.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=3 June 2026 |title=Super League forward makes month-long loan switch after arrival of fresh signing |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/rugby-league-news-super-league-forward-makes-month-long-loan-switch-115667/ |access-date=3 June 2026 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref>

==Playing style==
Beckett is known for his powerful carrying, high tackle count and work rate around the ruck, traits that helped him stand out in the Championship and earn a move to Super League. His physical presence in the front row offers Castleford greater depth and competition in the forward pack.<ref>[https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article/121/tigers-sign-jimmy-beckett Tigers sign Jimmy Beckett]</ref>
